prompt: What does the context window measure, and what does it include?
choices: The maximum tokens the model can consider at once, covering both input and output
Only the number of input tokens you are allowed to send
The model's permanent long-term memory of all past conversations
The maximum number of separate API calls allowed per minute

prompt: Why must an application re-supply prior conversation on every call?
choices: Because each API call is stateless — the model retains no memory between calls
Because re-sending makes the model respond faster
Because the provider deletes old messages after one minute
It doesn't — the model automatically remembers all earlier turns

prompt: Which of the following are valid strategies for staying within the context window? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Summarizing earlier conversation into a short recap
Retrieving only the most relevant pieces of a large corpus per request
Keeping only the most recent N turns (sliding window)

prompt: A BA is reviewing a feature where long chats start producing contradictory answers. Which fact best explains the root cause to the team?
choices: The model's temperature is set too low
As context grew past the window limit, older essential messages were dropped and no longer seen by the model
The model's training finished, so it stopped learning
Retrieval always corrupts the conversation history
